stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Netherlands to spend almost $300m on making drones for Ukraine
stories.summary: Nato promises not to lose sight of Ukraine conflict; Russia attacks Kyiv. What we know on day 1,512 The Netherlands will spend 248m euros ($293m) on producing drones for Ukraine, Dutch defence minister Dilan Yesilgoz-Zegerius said on Wednesday. “Drones play a crucial role on the modern battlefield. Ukrainians deploy them with incredible skill to repel the incessant Russian attacks,” she said after meeting her counterparts from Nato countries and the alliance’s secretary general Mark Rutte in Ber

stories.title: Orbán’s favorite Brussels think tank may be down, but it’s not out
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― The populist Brussels think tank beloved by Viktor Orbán faces a fight for survival after incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar vowed to cut funding — but its work will continue, its leaders say. The organization is braced to weather the political pressure, supporters and detractors of MCC Brussels told POLITICO, with alternative funding avenues, potential legal recourse and continued demand for its conservative Euroskeptic agenda expected to sustain it. “Without a doubt, the

stories.title: Does Viktor Orbán’s defeat signal a wider backlash against ‘the forces of darkness’?
stories.summary: Péter Magyar’s stunning victory in Hungary is a boost for liberal democracy. But don’t bank on similar upsets in upcoming European elections • Don’t get This Is Europe delivered to your inbox? Sign up here When future historians come to write about the stunning electoral overthrow of Viktor Orbán on 12 April 2026, let’s hope they devote at least footnotes to zebras and golden toilet brushes. The zebras were spotted by drones on the sprawling grounds of a countryside palace belonging to Orbán’s e

stories.title: Badenoch: Orbán’s defeat gives me hope for center right
stories.summary: LONDON — Viktor Orbán’s defeat in Sunday’s Hungarian election indicates that right-wing populism will be short-lived, the leader of Britain’s center-right Conservatives claimed. Péter Magyar’s decisive victory over Orbán — a close ally of both U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin — is “very positive,” Kemi Badenoch told Axel Springer CEO Mathias Döpfner in an interview for his podcast series MD Meets . (Axel Springer is the owner of

stories.title: Iran crisis shoves Europe’s economic woes off summit agenda
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Europe’s flagging economy is about to be bumped down the European Council agenda — again. Next week’s summit of EU leaders was supposed to be a chance to come up with responses to the economic malaise blighting the continent but instead it’s turning into another firefighting mission. The war in Iran is muscling out some of the longer-term issues. The meeting, on April 23-24 in Cyprus, was convened in large part to make progress on the EU’s €1.8 trillion seven-year bu

stories.title: Sinlaku rips through Northern Mariana Islands as strongest tropical cyclone this year
stories.summary: More than 1,000 people were in shelters across Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands as Sinlaku moved away Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Super Typhoon Sinlaku hammered the Northern Mariana Islands, flipping over cars, toppling utility poles and ripping away tin roofs. Authorities were just beginning to assess the damage left behind by the typhoon, which first hit the islands on Tuesday night local time and continued with a barrage of fierce winds a

stories.title: Hungary’s Péter Magyar says new government could take power at beginning of May
stories.summary: Magyar has called on Orbán's government to act as a caretaker in its final weeks and not to make any decisions that could threaten Hungary's interests or impede the incoming government's work.

stories.title: „Én riporter vagyok harminchárom éve, riporter, érti?”
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerdán két interjút is adott a közmédiának. Összeszedtük a legjobb pillanatokat.

stories.title: Hungarian troops to withdraw from key energy infrastructure sites, their presence no longer needed a few days after election
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán had deployed troops to guard key energy infrastructure in late February due to "concerns about a potential Ukrainian operation" targeting them.

stories.title: Hungary’s strongman lost. His ideas live on in the White House | Jamil Smith
stories.summary: The United States tolerates Trump’s behavior because of our warped definition of strength The strongest men I’ve known didn’t behave anything like Donald Trump. They were capable of restraint, first off. They may have spoken loudly, but they never used volume to enforce authority. None of them thought domination equaled leadership. How silly that would be. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hungary’s voters shunned Orbán – but it may be too early to celebrate end of Europe’s far right
stories.summary: Leaders of Poland and Germany hail Péter Magyar’s majority as a turning of the tide – but analysts say there were other reasons for defeat of prime minister For Poland’s Donald Tusk, the crushing defeat of Hungary’s illiberal prime minister, Viktor Orbán, after 16 years in office was evidence that the world was no longer “condemned to authoritarian and corrupt governments”. Germany’s chancellor, Friedrich Merz, also believes the two-thirds majority secured by Orbán’s centre-right challenger, Pét

stories.title: Hungary’s prime minister-elect vows to suspend ‘propaganda machine’ state media
stories.summary: Péter Magyar compares media coverage to Nazi-era Germany and aims to ‘restore its public service character’ Hungary’s prime minister-elect has vowed to suspend state media news coverage, describing it as a “propaganda machine,” when his government takes office around mid-May. Péter Magyar, whose landslide election victory on Sunday brought an end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power, detailed his plans for the suspension as he gave two tense interviews to public radio and television on Wednesday.

stories.title: How was Orbán defeated? With energetic campaigning and cunning exploitation of his weaknesses | Tibor Dessewffy
stories.summary: Péter Magyar did not need to dismantle the system – but he understood that Hungarians care more about the cost of living than conspiracies Hungary’s election delivered an unprecedented victory for Viktor Orbán’s challenger. With a record turnout of nearly 80% and a supermajority for the Tisza party of almost 70% of the seats, this was not merely a change of government: it was a change of regime, compressed into a single election night. After 16 years in power, Orbán became the victim of his own

stories.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
stories.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar hoping to form new government in early May
stories.summary: Hungary's new government could take power in just a few weeks after Peter Magyar met with President Solyuk. Magyar also called on the Orban-aligned president to step down.

stories.title: Crowds in Bratislava reject Fico plan to scrap overseas voting
stories.summary: Thousands rallied in Bratislava and other cities on Tuesday, 14 April 2026, to oppose Prime Minister Robert Fico’s plan to scrap mail voting for Slovaks abroad, warning it could undermine democratic participation.

stories.title: The human cost of the war in Sudan, three years on
stories.summary: The conflict, which erupted in 2023, has left behind a human toll which is "simply staggering", reports the BBC's Barbara Plett Usher.

stories.title: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.title: Vidnyánszky: A következő évad meghirdetésével egyelőre várunk
stories.summary: Szerdán derült ki, hogy a választás után rendkívüli társulati ülést hívott össze a Nemzeti Színház vezérigazgatója, akit ennek részleteiről kérdeztünk.

stories.title: Ligeti Miklós: A korrupció büntetlensége volt az Orbán által adott legnagyobb kegy
stories.summary: A Transparency International Magyarország jogi igazgatója a Szinten túlban az elszámoltatás és a vagyonvisszaszerzés lehetőségeiről beszélt. Krekó Péter a kormányra visszaütő orosz beavatkozást elemezte, D. Tóth Kriszta közös gyógyulást szeretne.

stories.title: Két fontos budapesti teret is felújítanak
stories.summary: Az Örs vezér tere és a Madách tér is megújulhat a XIV., illetve a VI. kerület által.

stories.title: „A magyar társadalom nagy többsége jól rezonál arra, hogy Sulyok Tamás távozzon a hivatalából”
stories.summary: A Political Capital szerdai konferenciáján a választásokat elemezve a tudomány győzelméről beszéltek a kutatók, és arról, hogy Orbán Viktort soha nem lehet leírni.

stories.title: Pénzmosást gyanít egy szerb ügyvéd az MVM terjeszkedése mögött, vizsgálatra buzdítja Magyar Pétert
stories.summary: A magyar állami cég tavaly nyáron jelentette be, hogy többségi tulajdonos lett a szerb Maneks Csoport két leányvállalatában. Szerintük a tranzakció minden előírásnak megfelelt, a cégértékelést független nemzetközi tanácsadó cégek végezték.

stories.title: A közmédia több tucat dolgozója levélben követeli, hogy állítsák vissza az MTI pártatlan hírszolgáltatását
stories.summary: A belső levélben Németh Zsolt hírigazgató felügyeleti jogának megszüntetését kérik.

stories.title: Végrehajtó voltam, Orbán a felelős – egy pillanat alatt megváltozott a hangulat a Karmelitában
stories.summary: A Tisza kétharmados győzelme után először ülésezett a leköszönő Orbán-kormány, mi pedig arra voltunk kíváncsiak, hogy a propaganda, az oroszbarátság és a korrupció mennyiben járulhatott hozzá a Fidesz történelmi vereségéhez.

stories.title: Elemzői értelmezésből lett Peszkovnak tulajdonított idézet, hogy a Kreml és Orbán sosem voltak barátok
stories.summary: Bár így terjedt el a sajtóban, úgy tűnik, nem hangzott el a Kreml szóvivőjétől, hogy „sosem voltunk barátok Orbánnal”, de a nyilatkozatokból ez valóban kiolvasható. Ez azonban nem valamiféle megtagadását jelenti az elődnek, hanem a Kreml üzemszerű működését, mert érdeke az együttműködés felmutatása.

stories.title: Vadul keresi az okokat a vereségre a fideszes közvélemény, és van, aki már eljutott a korrupcióig
stories.summary: Elindult a vereség feldolgozása vigasztaló, valóságtagadó és igazságkereső megszólalásokkal. Tényleg az irigység hajtotta az embereket, akik a kormány ellen szavaztak? Vagy nem kellett volna olyan fellengzősen és csúnyán beszélni? Vagy mindent jól csináltak, csak megunták őket?

stories.title: Orbánék mindig a személyes kapcsolatokat mantrázták, de barátai és szövetségesei sorban gratuláltak Magyarnak
stories.summary: Az oroszok pragmatikus kapcsolatot várnak, Kína kész szorosabbra fűzni a kapcsolatokat, Babiš és Erdoğan meghívta magához Magyart. Trump szerint az új kormányfő jó munkát fog végezni és jó ember.

stories.title: Bencsik András: Orbán Viktort az orránál fogva vezették a jobboldali közvélemény-kutatók
stories.summary: Szerinte hazudtak, ezért pedig mérhetetlenül haragszik rájuk.

stories.title: Beljebb osonna Trump kormányzatába Gorka Sebestyén
stories.summary: A magyar származású, terrorellenes szakértőként villogó Gorka Trump első kormánya idején nem szerzett sok rajongót a stílusával.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a közvetlen elnökválasztásról is beszélt Sulyok Tamással
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök a Sándor-palotánál tartott sajtótájékoztatón közölte, ha Sulyok nem távozik önként, akkor el fogják távolítani. Május elején megalakulhat az új országgyűlés.

stories.title: Még sokkal drágább lesz a mohácsi híd, hacsak meg nem fékezi az új kormány
stories.summary: A választások előtti utolsó Magyar Közlönyben tette közzé a kormány, hogy ötödével megemeli a már eddig is túlárazott projekt költségvetését. Mehet-e még plusz 76 milliárd a Duna Aszfaltnak?

stories.title: Volt kommunikációs igazgatója szerint Sulyok Tamás nem fog lemondani
stories.summary: Szántó Georgina arról is beszélt a Partizánnak, hogy mennyire megváltozott Sulyok Tamás, miután köztársasági elnök lett.

stories.title: Ők tartoznak a Tisza Párt kibővített vezetésébe
stories.summary: Kármán András megosztott egy fotót azokról, akik kedden részt vehettek a párt kibővített vezetői ülésén. Három nő, nagy hiányzók és meglepetésemberek a képen.

stories.title: Magyar: The new Hungarian Parliament expected to be formed in early May
stories.summary: The prime minister-elect also announced at a press conference after meeting with President Tamás Sulyok that he told him that if he does not step down himself, he will be removed.

stories.title: Jogvédett műsorok streameléséhez programozott egy magyar férfi Amerikában, az FBI közbenjárásával fogták el Magyarországon
stories.summary: Alig két hónapig tudott itt bújkálni.

stories.title: Feljelentették Orbánt, amiért vezetés közben mindkét kezével elengedte a kormányt, de nem indítanak eljárást
stories.summary: A BRFK arról tájékoztatta a Telexet, hogy nem szabálysértés, amit Orbán a videón csinált.

stories.title: Politico: Orbán kihagyja a következő EU-csúcsot
stories.summary: Von der Leyen el akar beszélgetni az uniós állam- és kormányfőkkel a kiszivárgott orosz–magyar egyeztetésekről, amire remek alkalmat adna a jövő heti ülés.

stories.title: Nádas Péter: Orbán az utóbbi években teljesen elvesztette a kapcsolatot a valósággal
stories.summary: A német Zeit a választás hétvégéjéről, Magyar Péter sikerének titkáról és a Fidesz bukásáról kérdezte a Kossuth-díjas írót.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban election loss reverberates across Central Europe
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

stories.title: After Orbán loss, Europe’s far right reassesses MAGA endorsement
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s defeat in the Hungarian elections suggests that political endorsements from Donald Trump or JD Vance do not necessarily boost European far-right campaigns, as the MAGA message increasingly struggles to resonate in Europe.

stories.title: What next for Hungary as country celebrates Péter Magyar victory over Viktor Orbán?
stories.summary: People in the capital, Budapest, and across Hungary celebrated the victory of the opposition party Tisza, led by Péter Magyar, which has won the parliamentary elections, putting an end to the 16-year rule of the populist Viktor Orbán. The Guardian's Europe reporter Jakub Krupa reports from the Hungarian capital Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban's election defeat sparks hope and relief
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major changes.

stories.title: EU applauds Hungary's election result
stories.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

stories.title: Hungarian voters celebrate 'much-needed' change
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by 45-year-old ex-party insider Péter Magyar.

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, international isolation and oligarchs amassing wealth. His landslide

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: Peace activist, 91, walks across Ireland in protest against US military stopovers
stories.summary: Lelia Doolan, who finished 220km trek at parliament gates, says use of Shannon airport violates Irish neutrality A 91-year-old peace activist has crossed Ireland on foot and arrived in Dublin to petition the government to bar US military flights. Lelia Doolan completed a two-week, 220km (138 mile) trek on Wednesday, ending at the gates of parliament accompanied by throngs of supporters. Continue reading...

stories.title: France seeks release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
stories.summary: Agents detained Marie-Therese Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to DHS The French government is pressing the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody after she was detained earlier this month. US Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) agents detained Marie-Therese Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to DHS. Ross is now being h

stories.title: House Democrats file six articles of impeachment against defense secretary Pete Hegseth – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Trump news at a glance: president renews threats against federal reserve chair, pushes his replacement Sign up for the Breaking News US email At a Turning Point USA event in Georgia on Tuesday, vice-president JD Vance was heckled by a protester who seemed to criticized the conflicts in the Middle East, including the war in Gaza. “Jesus Christ does not support genocide,” the audience member shouted. The vice-president addressed the demonstrator and agreed with their

stories.title: US Senate fails to pass war powers resolution for fourth time – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our latest full report is here: US and Iran in indirect talks to extend two-week ceasefire Donald Trump said the “special relationship” between the US and UK was in a poor state but that it will not have impact on King Charle’s upcoming state visit to America. In an interview with Sky News, the US president once again criticised Keir Starmer over his policies, particularly on energy and immigration, and reiterated his disappointment that the UK and other Nato allies had

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.summary: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Inside Pakistan’s turnaround with Trump
stories.summary: Even though U.S.-Iran talks collapsed this weekend, one country emerged as a winner on the world stage: Pakistan. Pakistan, which led the shuttle diplomacy between the two countries in recent weeks and hosted the talks in Islamabad, has raised its profile with the effort in a way that may pay dividends for years to come. “What they’ve done is put themselves on the map diplomatically,” said Elliott Abrams, who served as special representative for Iran during the first Trump administration. “I thi

stories.title: US navy plays deadly game of hide-and-seek with Iranian mines
stories.summary: The U.S. Navy has a critical new mission in the Strait of Hormuz: find and destroy any Iranian sea mines before they throw the global economy into further chaos. The threat is real: Iran’s stockpile of thousands of mines, while depleted by the U.S.-Israeli campaign, has not been completely destroyed. And U.S. military intelligence believes that some of those mines have already been deployed at sea, according to one U.S. official briefed on the matter, granted anonymity to discuss ongoing operati

stories.title: ‘Don’t lose sight of Ukraine,’ Nato chief tells European allies – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Meanwhile, Nato chief Mark Rutte urged members of the military alliance not to “lose sight” of the Ukraine conflict, and to boost their backing for Kyiv to $60bn in 2026, AFP reported. His comments came at the start of a meeting in Berlin of defence ministers from Ukraine’s key supporters, including Germany and Britain, with the conflict against Russia now in its fifth year. Continue reading...

stories.title: Democrats file articles of impeachment against Hegseth for ‘high crimes and misdemeanors’
stories.summary: Accusations refer to attack on Iran without congressional authorization and strikes on alleged drug smuggling boats House Democrats filed six articles of impeachment against Pete Hegseth on Wednesday, accusing the defense security of “high crimes and misdemeanors”, in reference to the attack on Iran without congressional authorization and deadly strikes on suspected drug smuggling boats, among other official acts. The move comes as the Trump administration faces mounting scrutiny over recent for

stories.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
stories.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

stories.title: En Europe, les fonctionnaires contraints de quitter WhatsApp
stories.summary: BRUXELLES — Les gouvernements européens ont décidé de se débarrasser de WhatsApp et de Signal. La France , l’Allemagne , la Pologne , les Pays-Bas , le Luxembourg et la Belgique ont commencé à mettre en place des services de messagerie internes permettant aux fonctionnaires d’échanger des informations sensibles. Le but est de les empêcher d’utiliser des applications chiffrées grand public et de passer à des alternatives locales qu’ils peuvent contrôler. L’ Otan dispose également de son propre se

stories.title: New EU entry-exit system causing up to three-hour delays, say airports
stories.summary: Airport body has asked for power to suspend EES checks requiring personal information and biometrics Travellers going through some European airports are reportedly waiting up to three hours at border checks because of the EU’s new entry-exit system (EES) . Passengers in airports in countries such as France, Germany, Belgium, Italy, Spain and Greece are waiting several hours at border checks, the Airports Council International (ACI) body has said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Palestinian leader Marwan Barghouti facing ‘escalating abuse’ in Israeli jails
stories.summary: ‘Palestine’s Mandela’ suffers three recent attacks including assault where prison guards set a dog on him, lawyer says The jailed Palestinian leader Marwan Barghouti is at immediate risk in Israeli jails, where he has been attacked three times in as many weeks, including in one assault last month where prison guards set a dog on the 66-year-old, his lawyer has said. Barghouti is often called Palestine’s Nelson Mandela. He is respected across otherwise feuding Palestinian factions, has broad popu

stories.title: Child victims of online sexual abuse in UK inadequately protected, review finds
stories.summary: Lack of funding leaving police forces failing to keep pace with two-thirds annual increase in referrals, says report Child victims of online sexual abuse are being inadequately protected from further harm because police forces are struggling to cope with an increase in this crime, his majesty’s chief inspector of constabulary has warned. Michelle Skeer said: “Without investment and coordination, the situation will worsen and children could be put at further risk.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Reeves gives more energy bill support to businesses as Iran war pushes up costs
stories.summary: Scheme cutting bills by up to 25% expanded to cover 10,000 firms, but they will not be paid until next year Rachel Reeves has announced an expansion of support for the most energy-intensive UK businesses, as they face soaring bills as a result of the Middle East conflict. The chancellor said the long-promised British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme (BICS) would be expanded to cover 10,000 companies, up from the 7,000 originally announced. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australia fuel watch tracker: check current petrol and diesel prices, service station outages and shipments – in charts
stories.summary: How much fuel does Australia have left today, and when could we run out? Track how much petrol and diesel prices have risen near you in Sydney, Melbourne and across the country. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The federal government has released fuel reserves, cut fuel excise taxes and rolled out a national fuel security plan as Australia battles a fuel crisis. While we know there have been outages and price increases, it can be difficult to get a full picture of wha

stories.title: Bernie Sanders’ effort to block US weapons sales to Israel fails in Senate
stories.summary: Senator’s fourth attempt for resolutions fails, but votes show growing appetite among Democrats to impose limits Bernie Sanders on Wednesday led a failed effort to block the sale of bombs and bulldozers to Israel, but the votes revealed a growing appetite among Democrats to impose limits on US weapons transfers to a longtime US ally. It was the fourth time Sanders, an independent who caucuses with Senate Democrats, had forced consideration of resolutions cutting off military aid for Israel in th

stories.title: Sonia Sotomayor apologizes to Brett Kavanaugh in US supreme court justice spat
stories.summary: In a spilling of the court’s divisions in public, Sotomayor had criticized Kavanaugh over a dissenting ruling on ICE raids Sonia Sotomayor, a US supreme court justice, issued an apology on Wednesday for her recent criticism of fellow justice Brett Kavanaugh, an unusual public mea culpa that underscores the continuing divisions within the nation’s top judicial body over its direction and actions in high-profile cases. Sotomayor had criticized Kavanaugh at an event in Kansas last week for an opini

stories.title: Former NSW MP spruiked as ‘esteemed’ Liberal at campaign event – despite Icac’s ‘serious corrupt conduct’ finding
stories.summary: Exclusive: John Sidoti wins cheers and applause at re-election fundraiser for his successor Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A former New South Wales minister found by Icac to have engaged in serious corrupt conduct has been feted as one of the “esteemed luminaries of the Liberal party” at an election fundraiser headlined by the state opposition leader, Kellie Sloane. John Sidoti, a former Liberal minister and sta

stories.title: Justice denied: why families of apartheid victims are still searching for answers
stories.summary: Struggle for justice symbolises limitations of Truth and Reconciliation Commission, whose hearings began 30 years ago Darkness had fallen on 27 June 1985 when Fort Calata, Matthew Goniwe, Sicelo Mhlauli and Sparrow Mkonto set off on the 150-mile drive back from a meeting of anti-apartheid activists in the South African city of Port Elizabeth, now known as Gqeberha. They never made it home. About an hour into their journey, as the road wound north from the coast towards their home town of Cradock

stories.title: The Brexit delusion is dead – so now Keir Starmer doesn’t need to pretend any more | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: To rebuild relations with Europe in a dangerous world, the prime minister needs to win big arguments, not hide behind outdated red lines In opposition, Keir Starmer pushed Brexit to the margin of debate. In government, he has learned that Europe is central to Britain’s interests whether you talk about it or not. The avoidance of painful arguments from the past turns out to be a handicap when making plans for the future. This was predictable. Labour’s 2024 general election manifesto pretended tha

stories.title: Short-term gains for China from US-Iran war may turn to longer-term pain
stories.summary: Beijing may be reaping some diplomatic benefit but Trump’s war holds risks for its energy security and economy Two months ago, China’s foreign minister, Wang Yi, promised it would be a “big year” for China-US relations. He was right, but perhaps not in the way he expected. Wang was speaking before a planned visit by the US president to Beijing in March, which would have been Donald Trump’s first trip to China since 2017. But the trip, and a meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping, was k

stories.title: Can Trump get a better Iran deal than Obama?
stories.summary: The 2015 Iran deal curbed Tehran's nuclear program until the US pulled out. After years of failed diplomacy and 40 days of war, talks are back on. Donald Trump has said he can strike a "better" deal than Barack Obama.

stories.title: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
stories.summary: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

stories.title: Former US Marine pilot loses appeal against extradition from Australia
stories.summary: Daniel Duggan was arrested in 2022 over claims he illegally trained the Chinese military in South Africa.

stories.title: Strikes hit Sloviansk and Sumy as Russia pounds Ukrainian cities
stories.summary: Russia strikes Sloviansk with a FAB-1500 bomb, wrecking civilian buildings, while repeated drone attacks ignite major fires in Sumy’s industrial zone.

stories.title: Sheinbaum vows to ‘defend Mexicans at every level’ amid anger at Trump over migrant deaths
stories.summary: Sheinbaum has recently been taking a firmer stance with the US, defying pressures where other countries have caved The Mexican government has voiced concern about the deaths of its citizens in US custody, with Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum also pushing back against the Trump administration’s decision to impose an energy blockade on Cuba. The progressive Mexican leader has walked a careful line with Trump for more than a year, addressing provocations with a measured tone and meeting US requ

stories.title: Amazon enters agreements for nine Australian renewable projects to power datacentres
stories.summary: Tech company has signed on to nine deals as it aims to reach net zero carbon emissions by 2040 Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Amazon has entered power agreements with nine new renewable projects in New South Wales and Victoria, as the technology company seeks to source renewable power for its datacentre operations in Australia. The nine deals, including one windfarm and 10 solar and battery projects, will take t

stories.title: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
stories.summary: Appointment of Roelf Meyer seen as attempt to improve relations amid false US accusations of ‘white genocide’ South Africa has appointed a former apartheid government chief negotiator during the talks that ended white rule in the 1990s as ambassador to the US, in what is seen as an attempt to improve the deeply strained diplomatic relationship between the two countries. Roelf Meyer replaces Ebrahim Rasool, who was expelled in March 2025 after he criticised the Trump administration. Continue read

stories.title: More than £1bn pledged for Sudan as humanitarian crisis deepens
stories.summary: Donors exceed funding target at Berlin conference but prospects for ceasefire remain distant More than £1bn (€1.15bn) has been pledged for war-ravaged Sudan at a conference in Berlin, eclipsing the funding target organisers had set to help mitigate the world’s largest humanitarian crisis. The financial commitments made on Wednesday will also help offset a chronic humanitarian funding shortfall in a country devastated by three years of conflict, where two-thirds of its population – 34m people – r

stories.title: Trump needs a better Iran deal than Obama’s – but faces major hurdles
stories.summary: US president will need to show heavy costs of war were worthwhile while Iran must choose between instant and delayed gratification Middle East crisis – live updates If talks between Iran and the US reconvene within the next few days in Islamabad, Donald Trump will have two major political hurdles to overcome – first showing that any deal he secures is better than the one signed by Barack Obama in 2015 and from which he withdraw in 2018, and secondly proving the deal is more favourable than the o

stories.title: MPs vote against social media ban for under-16s a second time
stories.summary: Commons rejects proposal by 256 to 150 to side with government on plan to tackle online harms affecting children MPs have rejected a proposal to ban under-16s from using social media for the second time, as the prime minister summoned tech bosses to demand tougher action on internet safety. The House of Commons sided with the government against a Lords amendment to the children’s wellbeing and schools bill that imposed a new age limit on using social media platforms, amid pressure from parents a

stories.title: Two arrested over attempted arson attack on synagogue in north London
stories.summary: Woman, 47, and man, 46, held on suspicion of arson endangering life after attempted Finchley attack A 47-year-old woman and a 46-year-old man have been arrested on suspicion of arson endangering life after an attempted attack on a synagogue in Finchley, north London, as part of an investigation into what the Metropolitan police described as an “antisemitic hate crime”. The force said the woman was arrested at an address in Watford just after 4.45pm on Wednesday, while the man was arrested at 7.1

stories.title: Drax claimed record £999m in subsidies for burning trees in 2025, thinktank says
stories.summary: Company has received about £8.7bn in renewable energy subsidies since 2012, despite claims wood pellets are not sourced sustainably The owner of the Drax power plant in North Yorkshire received record subsidies of almost £1bn for burning trees to generate electricity in 2025, a climate thinktank has calculated. The company was paid £999m last year for generating about 4.5% of Great Britain’s electricity from its biomass plant, costing each household £13 a year, according to analysts at Ember. Co

stories.title: Georgia man charged over attacks that killed DHS worker and another woman
stories.summary: Killings of Lauren Bullis and woman not yet identified in ‘random’ Monday attacks draw attention of Trump officials An Atlanta man has been charged in a string of attacks over a matter of hours that left two women dead and a man in critical condition, drawing the Trump administration’s attention after one of the victims was identified as a Department of Homeland Security (DHS) employee who was walking her dog. The killing of the DHS worker, Lauren Bullis, and shootings of the two other victims o

stories.title: Geelong fire: fuel supply fears after out-of-control blaze at one of Australia’s two remaining oil refineries
stories.summary: Petrol production affected at Viva plant in Corio as mayor calls blaze ‘unprecedented’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An explosive fire at a Geelong oil refinery – which supplies half of Victoria’s fuel and 10% of the nation’s – has broken out, with petrol production to be affected for some time and authorities warning the full extent of the damage is still unknown. The blaze at the Viva Energy facility in Corio

stories.title: When the ‘Dubai dream’ goes wrong - podcast
stories.summary: Journalist Will Coldwell tells the story of how a British businessman was imprisoned in Dubai – and how his family finally got him home When Albert Douglas found out he was facing a long prison sentence in Dubai, he tried to escape the UAE … and failed. What followed was years of court proceedings, time in prison and even, Douglas says, beatings and torture. In recent years, scores of business owners, unsuspecting tourists and influencers have been detained in Dubai – caught up in an opaque lega

Shortly before 7am, he said about 50 firefighting vehicles were continuing to fight the blaze in the Moga

stories.title: Ant smuggler sentenced to a year in jail by Kenyan court
stories.summary: More than 2,200 ants were found in Zhang Kequn’s luggage at Nairobi airport, with baggage destined for China A Chinese national has been sentenced to a year in prison and fined by a Nairobi court for attempting to smuggle thousands of ants out of Kenya, a lucrative trade in east Africa that was exposed last year. The insects are mostly destined for China, the US and Europe, where they become pets and can be worth about $100 each. Continue reading...

stories.title: German health minister announces billions in cutbacks
stories.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

stories.title: South Africa appoints veteran Afrikaner politician Roelf Meyer as US ambassador amid tense Trump ties
stories.summary: South Africa has appointed a prominent Afrikaner politician, Roelf Meyer, to the ambassadorial role in Washington. This comes amid allegations from the Trump administration of a "white genocide" in the country.

stories.title: Germany: Sudan aid conference in Berlin raises €1.3B
stories.summary: The war in Sudan is entering its fourth year with international attention focused on other conflicts. The conference in Berlin is aiming to bring back some attention to help fund urgent projects.

stories.title: Berlin hosts Sudan Conference: a brutal, forgotten conflict
stories.summary: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope to at least ease the suffering of the people.

stories.title: Firefighters battle huge blaze at Australian oil refinery
stories.summary: The fire is burning out of control at the Geelong oil refinery, one of only two in Australia.

stories.title: UK signs deal to rejoin EU's Erasmus exchange scheme
stories.summary: Britain will rejoin the popular Europe-wide student exchange program six years after leaving it.

stories.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
stories.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

stories.title: Iran: Hundreds of thousands of jobs lost due to war
stories.summary: After six weeks of war, job losses are growing in Iran. Destroyed industrial facilities have brought production in many sectors to a standstill, hitting Iranian workers particularly hard.

stories.title: What's behind Israel's attacks along Lebanon's Litani River?
stories.summary: Israel is pushing forward with plans to remove Lebanese homes and residents from between the Litani River and its own border, creating a buffer zone. Why is the area strategically important in the Middle East conflict?

stories.title: Chinese national given one year in prison for smuggling ants out of Kenya
stories.summary: Zhang Kequn was arrested in March while trying to travel to China with more than 2,000 live ants.

stories.title: Prominent Palestinian prisoner Marwan Barghouti assaulted three times in a month, family says
stories.summary: The Israeli Prison Service says the allegation that Barghouti was subjected to physical violence by prison guards are "false and baseless".

stories.title: EU pursues 'deeper and stronger' partnerships with Gulf states, Costa tells Euronews in Doha
stories.summary: In the highest-level EU visit to the Gulf since the Iran war started, European Council President Antonio Costa reinforced the EU’s strategic partnerships with the Gulf states and pushed for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, warning of “disastrous consequences”.

stories.title: BBC to cut up to 2,000 jobs in next two years to save €575ml from operating costs
stories.summary: The BBC is funded by the public paying for a licence to view or listen to content and the corporation says 94% of UK adults use its services every month.

stories.title: SantaCon organiser charged with stealing $1m from charity pub crawl
stories.summary: Prosecutors say Stefan Pildes spent hundreds of thousands of $2.7m raised for charity on personal expenses.

stories.title: Frank Gardner: What is China's role in the Iran war?

stories.title: North Korea showing 'serious increase' in ability to make nuclear weapons, IAEA says
stories.summary: North Korea, which conducted its first nuclear test in 2006, is under rafts of UN sanctions for its banned weapons programmes.

stories.title: 'Discussions are being had' on second round of Iran talks in Pakistan, White House says
stories.summary: Negotiators are focusing on three key points that derailed last weekend's talks, including Iran's nuclear programme, the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for wartime damages.

stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.summary: The lawsuit said the firm's practices had led to higher ticket prices and worse service for customers.

stories.title: Peru election dispute deepens amid slow ballot count

stories.title: 250 missing after migrant boat sinks in Indian Ocean
stories.summary: The trawler "reportedly sank due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding", the United Nations said.

stories.title: Viral post comparing Portugal's Hormuz conquest to US Iran operation mixes facts with exaggeration

stories.summary: At Ukrainian-German government talks, Berlin reassures Kyiv it can continue counting on military, civilian, and humanitarian aid, even as political opposition and US cuts create challenges for sustained support.

stories.title: North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capability, UN watchdog warns
stories.summary: Pyongyang making ‘very serious’ progress on producing weapons, with rapid rise in activity at main nuclear complex North Korea has made “very serious” progress in its ability to produce more nuclear weapons, the head of the UN’s nuclear watchdog has said, in another sign that the regime is seeking to use its nuclear arsenal to ensure its survival . North Korea is thought to have assembled about 50 nuclear warheads, although some experts are sceptical of its claims that it is able to miniaturise

stories.title: Tax Day is a reminder of America’s unequal tax system. But we can fix it | Zohran Mamdani, Gabriel Zucman and Joseph Stiglitz
stories.summary: There is no justification for a regressive system in which the super-rich contribute less than the rest of us Today, we have more income and wealth inequality than ever before. New York City’s average household income is $131,000 . Without extreme inequality, residents could live reasonably well. Instead, a few people at the top of the income ladder capture enormous wealth, while millions of others struggle just to get by. Some simply can’t make it. For them, New York has become fundamentally un

stories.title: Paris art enthusiast wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
stories.summary: Ari Hodara initially thought it might be a hoax after winning raffle he found out about by chance while dining out A Picasso painting worth more than €1m (£870,000) has been won in a raffle by a software engineer from Paris who thought the whole thing might be a hoax. Ari Hodara learned he was the winner of the raffle on Tuesday when he answered a video call from Christie’s auction house in Paris. “How do I check that it’s not a hoax?” the 58 year-old asked when he was told he was the new owner

stories.title: Bemutatjuk az élelmiszerboltot, ahol a vevők egyben tulajdonosok és alkalmazottak is
stories.summary: Befizetik a kauciót, ledolgoznak havonta három órát, de beleszólhatnak az áruválasztékba, és olcsóbban juthatnak jobb minőségű élelmiszerekhez.

stories.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
stories.summary: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

stories.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
stories.summary: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

stories.title: India's aviation boom hits turbulence amid Iran war
stories.summary: From longer routes to higher fares, the Middle East conflict is exposing vulnerabilities in India's fast-growing airline sector.

stories.summary: In the new DW documentary "Democracy Under Attack: Can Dündar and Trump's America," the Turkish press freedom icon looks at the parallels between the erosion of democracy in the US and his home country.

stories.title: EU chief urges bloc-wide push on age verification app to protect children online
stories.summary: EU chief calls for a bloc-wide push on an age verification app to protect children online. If enforced, users will have to prove their age to access legally restricted sites.

stories.title: Watch: Vance heckled as he chides Pope

stories.title: South Korea jails American YouTuber for public nuisance

stories.title: Australia's richest person must share part of her mining fortunes, court rules

stories.title: In Annaba, Pope Leo XIV hails Algeria’s small Catholic flock and Saint Augustine
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV celebrates Mass at Annaba’s Basilica of Saint Augustine, highlighting Algeria’s small Catholic community during his symbolic visit.

stories.title: Australian pleads guilty to creating deepfake porn in landmark case
stories.summary: The 19-year-old is the first person to be charged under a new national law.

stories.title: Trump accuses Italy’s Meloni of not having 'courage' to back Iran war
stories.summary: Donald Trump attacks European ally Meloni for refusing to back the war on Iran and for defending Pope Leo XIV, sparking a rare show of unity among Italian parties.

stories.title: Pakistan hosts thousands of Sikh pilgrims for 2026 baisakhi rites
stories.summary: Pakistan welcomed around 2,300 Sikh pilgrims from India for Baisakhi celebrations at Gurdwara Panja Sahib in Hasan Abdal, about 45 kilometres from Islamabad.

stories.title: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
stories.summary: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

stories.title: New video shows moment Orion capsule hatch opens after splashdown
stories.summary: New footage shows the excitement when US Navy divers reach and open the hatch on the Orion capsule of Artemis II reaching the astronauts for the first time in 10 days after their trip to the moon.

stories.title: Berlin conference seeks urgent aid as Sudan war fuels mass poverty
stories.summary: Germany hosts a Berlin conference on Wednesday to mobilise aid for Sudan’s worsening war-driven crisis, focusing on civilian needs as hunger and poverty soar.

stories.title: Moment gunman tackled by school principal
stories.summary: CCTV footage shows a high school principal charging towards a former student who was armed in Oklahoma.

stories.title: Kenya fuel prices rise sharply despite reduction in tax due to Iran war
stories.summary: Diesel prices rise by a record margin despite a reduction in value added tax.

stories.title: Three years of messages at once - a chronicle of Sudan's war pours in as trapped reporter's phone turns on
stories.summary: As the conflict enters its fourth year, journalist Mohamed Suleiman shudders at what has been lost.

stories.title: Europe's responsibility in the world (dis)order
stories.summary: The EU must be able to act. The principle of unanimity in foreign and security policy is an obstacle to this. A "European Security Council" could change this, say Hans-Gert Pöttering, former President of the European Parliament, and Klaus Welle, Martens Centre for European Studies.

stories.title: China set new records in trade and investment in Central Asia in 2025
stories.summary: China's trade with five Central Asian countries hit a record in 2025 — and the relationship has moved well beyond buying and selling. Chinese companies are now partners in strategic projects across high-tech industries including energy and mining.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.summary: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Meghan to appear as guest judge on MasterChef Australia

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Gaza’s Al‑Shati camp and Al‑Shifa Hospital
stories.summary: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Gaza’s Al‑Shati camp and Al‑Shifa Hospital area despite a ceasefire, with at least 11 Palestinians killed in renewed violence.

stories.title: Trump's rift with Pope is playing out in public - it's costing him valuable support
stories.summary: Leading conservative Catholics tell the BBC why they back the American pontiff in his spat with Trump.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war 'very close to over' as truce talks advance
stories.summary: A second round of talks between Washington and Tehran could take place within days, according to Trump. Meanwhile, Israel continues to press ahead with its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.

stories.title: South Africa names apartheid-era politician as new ambassador to the US
stories.summary: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.

stories.title: UN nuclear chief urges checks of Iran's programme in potential deal to end war
stories.summary: Tehran has not allowed the IAEA access to its nuclear facilities bombed by Israel and the US during a 12-day conflict in June, according to a confidential IAEA report.

stories.title: Mi tesszük szörnnyé a szörnyeket
stories.summary: A Frankenstein – Él! Él! azt mutatja be, hogy jut el az öngyilkosság széléről a létezés elfogadásáig egy kitaszított. A Mary Shelley őshorrorját folytató képregényből kiderül, hogy egy rettegett rém is lehet áldozat.

stories.title: Felpörgette az olajválság az elektromos autók európai eladásait
stories.summary: A kínai gyártók igyekeznek is kihasználni a lehetőséget, Olaszországban például már az újautó-piac 40 százalékát szerezték meg.

stories.title: Amelyik filmnek már megítélték az állami támogatást, az meg is fogja azt kapni a Filmintézet szerint
stories.summary: A Filmintézet vezetőinek sorsáról az új kormány dönt, Káel Csaba pedig az új kormány felállásáig marad kormánybiztos.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergelynek előre megmondta a felesége, hogy el fogják veszíteni a választást, de ő nem hitt neki
stories.summary: Havasi Bertalan viszont már a kampány alatt is a Medián számait tartotta reálisnak.

stories.title: „Lovagolhatunk a szavakon, én nem hívnám ezt öncenzúrának, de van egy ilyen kifejezés, hogy szerkesztői szabadság”
stories.summary: Így védekezett az MTVA csatornaigazgatója a dolgozók cenzúravádjával szemben egy hozzánk eljutott hangfelvétel szerint. Többször előfordult, hogy helyettes államtitkári szintről érkeztek az okosságok – mondta egy újságíró az MTVA szerdai állománygyűlésén.

stories.title: Életfogytiglanra ítélték a marina parti gyilkosokat
stories.summary: K. Norbertet negyed évszázad múlva feltételesen szabadlábra helyezhetik, H. Zsoltot soha.

stories.title: 11 szavazattal kevesebbel, de az újraszámlálás szerint is a Tisza Párt jelöltje vezet a keszthelyi választókerületben
stories.summary: Jelen állás szerint 48 szavazat a különbség Varga Balázs javára a fideszes Nagy Bálinttal szemben, de a végleges eredményt leghamarabb péntek este, az átjelentkező és külképviseleti szavazók szavazatainak megszámlálása után állapítják meg.

stories.title: Dézsi Csaba András: A vádlott azt akarja bebizonyítani, hogy én voltam a maffiafőnök
stories.summary: A győri hulladékgazdálkodási ügy két vádlottja a mai tárgyaláson azt állította, hogy politikai parancsra kötöttek előnytelen szerződéseket. A tanúként meghallgatott Dézsi Csaba András volt polgármester szerint ez nonszensz.

stories.title: Cser-Palkovics szerint szembenézésre, utána pedig teljesen új elnökségre van szükség a Fideszben

stories.title: Nem fog engedni a háborús nyomásnak, mondta Starmer, miután Trump belengette, bármikor meg lehet változtatni a vámmegállapodást
stories.summary: Trump az utóbbi időben többször bírálta a brit miniszterelnököt, amiért az következetesen ki akar maradni a közel-keleti konfliktusból.

stories.title: Az orosz titkosszolgálatokhoz köthető diplomatát fogadott szerdán Kubatov Gábor fia
stories.summary: A nemzetközi szervek főosztályát vezető Kubatov Marcell Pavel Cvetovot, az orosz követség elsőtitkárát fogadta.

stories.title: Nagy István agrárminiszter három nappal a választási vereség után elárulta, kiket tart a jövő reménységeinek
stories.summary: Nagy István azután posztolt, hogy Cser-Palkovics András azt nyilatkozta: a Fidesznek teljesen új elnökségre van szüksége.

stories.title: Donald Trump szerint a diétás üdítőitalok megölik a rákos sejteket
stories.summary: Az érvelése szerint a diétás üdítő jót tesz neki, mert ha a fűre öntik, kiégeti a füvet, tehát akkor biztos a szervezetben is hasonló hatást gyakorol a rákos sejtekre.

stories.title: Több mint 70 ezer milliárd forinttal emelne a készülő uniós költségvetésen az EP szakbizottsága
stories.summary: Többet fordítanának például a tervezett versenyképességi alapra, a Horizont Európa kutatási programra és az Erasmus+ oktatási cserékre.

stories.title: 4–3-as, felejthetetlen meccsen jutott tovább a Bayern a Real ellen a BL elődöntőjébe
stories.summary: A madridiak háromszor vezettek, de a Bayern az utolsó pillanatokban fordított. A PSG-vel játszik az elődöntőben, az Arsenal az Atléticóval.

stories.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after byelection win
stories.summary: Carney’s Liberals will now be able to pass legislation without the support of opposition parties – and govern until 2029 The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has secured a parliamentary majority for his Liberal government, CBC News reported. The victory will help him push through a legislative agenda he says is needed for an increasingly divided geopolitical world. Three byelections were held on Monday in Ontario and Quebec, with two in districts – known as ridings – that have long voted Li

stories.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
stories.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

stories.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.

stories.title: Greek police using masked migrants to forcibly push other migrants back across border
stories.summary: Mercenaries recruited since at least 2020 for illegal migrant ‘pushbacks’ in Greece, BBC told

stories.title: Trump refuses to apologise after clash with Pope Leo XIV over Iran war
stories.summary: Despite widespread criticism including Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni's rare rebuke after the US president lambasted the US-born pontiff for being "weak on crime," Trump said no to an apology to the Catholic Church's Holy Father while reiterating his claims.

stories.title: Sixteen injured in rare Turkey school shooting in Siverek district
stories.summary: Turkey was rocked by a school shooting on Tuesday 14 April 2026 when an 18 year old former student opened fire at Ahmet Koyuncu Vocational and Technical Anatolian High School in Siverek, in Şanlıurfa province.

stories.title: Espionage, sabotage, attacks: German intelligence agency sends warning to defence industry
stories.summary: In March, the Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution is said to have approached the defence industry to press for stricter security standards.

stories.title: Berlin and Kyiv seal sweeping pact on drones, data and missiles
stories.summary: According to the Ukrainian president, who visited the German capital, at least 10 agreements were reached at the German-Ukrainian government consultations in Berlin - the first since 2004.

stories.title: Bahamas police release Michigan man questioned after wife disappeared from their boat
stories.summary: Brian Hooker told police that Lynette Hooker fell overboard and that strong currents carried her away Police in the Bahamas have released without charges a Michigan man who said his wife disappeared after falling overboard from a small boat in waters off the Caribbean island country, authorities said on Monday. Brian Hooker, of Onsted in southern Michigan, had been in police custody since 8 April – five days – after being questioned by authorities. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK steel exports to EU at risk as bloc doubles tariffs and halves quotas
stories.summary: Decision to reduce duty-free quotas by 47% aimed at curbing Chinese imports The EU is to go ahead with plans to double tariffs and halve quotas on imports of steel from July, in a move designed to curb Chinese imports but which could damage UK exports to the bloc. The decision by EU lawmakers and member states after late night talks on Monday, will reduce duty-free quotas by 47%. Exact country allocations have yet to be determined. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK woman fears being stranded in Spain over new Home Office border rules
stories.summary: Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa was refused boarding on flight to London because she was not aware of the rule change A British woman has told how she fears being stranded in Spain for months after being refused boarding a on flight back home to London because she was not aware of new Home Office border rules. Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa, 26, was born in the UK to a British father and a Spanish mother, but because of archaic laws she was not entitled automatically to British citizenship because her

stories.title: Chiang Mai’s New Year revelry hit by smog and war-related price spikes
stories.summary: Air pollution caused by wildfires is another blow to northern Thailand’s tourism industry as businesses suffer amid war in Iran The Doi Suthep temple in northern Thailand is known for its spectacular views of Chiang Mai and the lush forested mountains that surround it. Over recent weeks, though, visitors can see little of the city beyond a thick cloud of grey haze. Persistent wildfires have caused intense air pollution across the north of Thailand, forcing three provinces to declare emergencies

stories.title: Air New Zealand's economy Skynest bunk beds set for launch
stories.summary: Passengers can book a four-hour session in the bunk beds from May for Auckland-New York flights but airline cautions against smuggling in children Economy passengers on Air New Zealand’s ultra-long-haul flight between Auckland and New York can book a spot in the airline’s bunk-bed style sleeping pods from May, which will take to skies in late 2026. In what the airline says is a world first, six full-length, lie-flat sleeping pods, are squeezed into the aisle of the new Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner. T

stories.title: Két napon belül a második iskolai lövöldözés történt Törökországban, négyen meghaltak
stories.summary: Az iskola egy diákja nyitott tüzet, az egyik tanár és ő is az áldozatok között van.

stories.title: Az MTVA csatornaigazgatója a dolgozóknak: Aki nem tudja magában tartani az eredményekkel kapcsolatos érzéseit, erre a hónapra szabadságra mehet
stories.summary: Szerdán állománygyűlést tartottak a közmédia Kunigunda utcai központjában.

stories.title: Donald Trump az iráni háborúról: Már nagyon közel a vége
stories.summary: Az USA hétfőn megkezdett blokádjára Irán szerdán úgy reagált, nem fogják engedélyezni „semmi export vagy import folytatását a Perzsa-öbölben, az Ománi-tengeren és a Vörös-tengeren”.

stories.title: Jellinek Dániel, az ország egyik leggazdagabb embere egyetért a Tisza vagyonadó-tervével
stories.summary: De úgy látja, még sok kulcskérdést kell tisztázni ahhoz, hogy az intézkedés ne váljon kontraproduktívvá.

stories.title: Fogy a pénz, de a Tisza-ígéretek szerint a Fidesz-kormány után is lesznek felújítási programok
stories.summary: Az Otthonfelújítási Programot felfüggesztik a fővárosban és hét vármegyében. A Tisza Párt programjában családi- és társasházak energetikai felújítására is van ígéret.

stories.title: Levonulnak a katonák az energetikai létesítményektől, a választás után pár nappal már nincs szükség a megerősítésükre
stories.summary: A Honvéd Vezérkar Kommunikációs Főnöksége szerint a kritikus infrastruktúrát üzemeltető cégek „saját erőből fokozták őrzés-védelmüket”.

stories.title: Katy Perry szerint kamu, hogy szexuálisan zaklatta Ruby Rose-t, de az ausztrál rendőrség nyomozást indított
stories.summary: Az ausztrál színésznő pár napja azt állította, a húszas éveik elején szexuálisan bántalmazta az énekesnő egy melbourne-i szórakozóhelyen.

stories.title: A Siket című film nyerte a 2026-os LUX közönségdíjat
stories.summary: Az Európai Parlament díját elnyert film arról szól, milyen kihívások elé állít egy siket nőt a várandósság és a gyereknevelés.

stories.title: This is the Tisza Party's broader leadership
stories.summary: András Kármán shared a photo of those who attended the meeting of the party’s broader leadership on Tuesday. There are three women in the group, some notable absentees, and a few surprising faces.

stories.title: Szexjátékokat vásárolt a Mészároshoz köthető V-Híd költségére a cég egyik dolgozója
stories.summary: De a számlát sztornózhatták, miután egy másik dolgozó értesítette a HR-osztályt a megrendelésről.

stories.title: A Duna TV-n állandó vendég Pásztor Anna szerint Tóth Gabi pár hónapja felhívta, hogy be kéne mennie a frakcióba beszélgetni Kocsis Mátéval
stories.summary: Az Anna and the Barbies énekesnője, aki állandó vendég a közmédia egyik műsorában, a Telexnek azt mondta, a választás után sokan megkönnyebbültek a műsor készítői közül.

stories.title: Cser-Palkovicsot kommentig bőszítette az MLSZ az osztályozós döntésével
stories.summary: A székesfehérvári polgármester a Facebookon osztotta ki Csányi Sándor szövetségét.

stories.title: Spanish PM Sanchez' wife charged with corruption
stories.summary: The case is based on a complaint by an anti-corruption group with far-right ties, and is the latest in a string of corruption allegations which have plagued Sanchez' inner circle.

stories.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
stories.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

stories.title: Sudan: Iran war prompts 'massive' food, fuel price rises; drones kill hundreds of civilians in 2026
stories.summary: German charities warn of increases of 70-80% in the costs of essentials in wartorn Sudan amid the unrest in the Middle East. Donors gather in Berlin on Wednesday on the third anniversary of the outbreak of the war.

stories.title: Lufthansa pilots to strike again for 2 more days, leading to a full working week of disruption
stories.summary: Pilots at Germany's Lufthansa airline will strike again on Thursday and Friday, the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union said. Cabin crew are also striking this week, clouding the company's centenary celebrations

stories.title: France's Renault slashes engineer jobs amid pressure from Chinese carmakers
stories.summary: The French carmaker said it would cut up to 2,400 engineering jobs as the company tries to catch up to Chinese electric vehicles in terms of price and production speed.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates historic Mass at rain‑soaked Basilica in Algiers
stories.summary: Algeria hosted Pope Leo XIV on Monday 14 April for a Mass at the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers, marking the first papal visit to the country.

stories.title: Spain seeks closer China links amid global tensions
stories.summary: Pedro Sánchez meets Xi Jinping in Beijing on Tuesday 14 April 2026, aiming to strengthen political and trade relations at a time of global tension.
